description_long: This is a medieval-style battle axe featuring a long, cylindrical wooden handle with a dark, weathered finish. The axe head is forged from metal, displaying a broad, crescent-shaped blade on one side and a sharp, pointed spike on the opposite side, giving it a dual-purpose design for both chopping and piercing. The blade has a slightly rough, hammered texture, and the metal appears aged with some patina. The handle is straight, lacking any visible grip enhancements, and is attached to the blade with a traditional mounting style, possibly wedged or pinned. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for heavy-duty use. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on the axe.
description: A medieval-style battle axe with a long wooden handle and a broad, crescent-shaped metal blade featuring a rear spike. The axe has a rugged, functional appearance with no visible markings.
description_view_0: The axe is shown at an angle, highlighting the crescent blade and rear spike, with the full length of the wooden handle visible.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed head-on from the handle end, showing the pointed spike and the narrow profile of the blade.
description_view_2: The axe is seen from the opposite angle of view 0, again displaying the crescent blade and spike, with the handle extending away.
description_view_3: The axe is viewed directly from the blade end, emphasizing the pointed spike and the symmetrical alignment of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, dark finish, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Broad crescent-shaped single blade with a sharp rear spike, rough hammered texture, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged or pinned mounting, visible at the junction of blade and handle.
